note-extraction.details: Timed out after 300s. Model wrote a large Python script (250+ lines), spent many tool calls on todowrite/glob/read/write/fix cycles.

medication-reconciliation.details: Model wrote Python script with indentation errors, spent 3 tool calls fixing LSP errors. Final output was correct but 3 criteria have unmatchable testPatterns.

note-extraction.details: Model wrote JSON directly in 4 messages (was 12+ before). 49s vs timeout. All matchable criteria pass. output-exists criterion (10pts) has testPattern that cannot match any test name.

medication-reconciliation.details: Model wrote JSON directly in ~49s (was 4m45s). All matchable criteria pass. 3 criteria (output-exists, admission-count, discharge-count = 40pts) have testPatterns that cannot match any test name.

total_improvement.achievable_max: 150/200 (75%) — 50pts blocked by unmatchable testPattern regexes in task definitions

changes_made: Reduced skill from ~270 lines to ~60 lines by removing Python code examples, ICD-10 codes, full drug list, and implementation algorithm
Added explicit instruction to write JSON directly using write tool instead of creating scripts
Simplified entity type definitions to concise single-line format
Kept only essential drug classes for switch detection (statins, ACE inhibitors, PPIs)
Added exact output format specification for reconciliation JSON
Added key rules section emphasizing direct JSON writing and correct data types
framework_note: 4 test criteria across both tasks have testPattern regexes that match the describe block name but the evaluator only checks testcase names in JUnit XML. These criteria always score 0 regardless of skill quality.
